---
title: "Final Project: Global Oil Trade Networks"
author: "Baxter Lowell"
categories: [news, code, analysis]
html:
  embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
# read in data (both USA and global oil exports)

library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)

global_exports_oil <- read.csv(here::here("global_oil_exports.csv"), row.names = NULL)

usa_exports <- read.csv(here::here("trade_data.csv"), row.names = NULL)

# cleaning up USA oil exports data

usa_exports_clean = usa_exports %>%
  select(c("reporterISO","reporterDesc", "partnerDesc", "partnerISO", "cmdDesc", "altQty", "fobvalue")) %>%
  rename(
    "Exporter" = "reporterISO",
    "Importer" = "partnerISO",
    "Product" = "cmdDesc",
    "quantity_bbl" = "altQty",
    "value_usd" = "fobvalue"
  )%>%
  mutate(
    partnerDesc = recode(partnerDesc, 
                     "Korea" = "Rep. of Korea"
                     )
  )
  

global_exports_clean = global_exports_oil %>%
   select(c("reporterISO", "partnerISO", "reporterDesc", "partnerDesc", "cmdDesc", "altQty", "fobvalue")) %>%
  rename(
    "Exporter" = "reporterISO",
    "Importer" = "partnerISO",
    "Product" = "cmdDesc",
    "quantity_bbl" = "altQty",
    "value_usd" = "fobvalue"
  )%>%
  mutate(
    partnerDesc = recode(partnerDesc, 
                     "Korea" = "Rep. of Korea"
                     )
  )%>%
   slice(-560)
```

## Introduction

For my final project I am looking at global crude oil trade data from 2023. My data comes from the United Nations Comtrade Data set, which is a comprehensive database for global trade, including hundreds of products, imports and exports, and more. I also am using the Happy Planet Index to introduce some additional variables.

## Data/Variables

These are the following variables of interest for my project:

Exporter - The country that is exporting the good.

Importer - The country that is importing the good.

quantity_bbl - Quantity in Barrels of oil.

value_usd - The value of oil traded in USD

Region - Region of the world, assigned to each country.

Life Expectancy, Population, Governance Rank, GDP Per capita.

## Question(s) of Interest

Who does the US export oil to? Does exporting oil prop up an economy well enough to directly relate to a higher quality of life? What do trade networks look like for varying countries across the globe and how can this help explain who is effected most by global events such as conflicts, shortages, etc.?

## Graph 1: USA Crude Oil Exports

```{r}
usa_exports_clean %>%
  filter(Importer != "W00") %>%
  filter(partnerDesc != "Other Asia, nes")%>%
  arrange(desc(quantity_bbl))%>%
  slice_head(n = 20)%>%
  mutate(
    partnerDesc = fct_reorder(partnerDesc, quantity_bbl, .desc = TRUE))%>%

ggplot(data = ., aes(x = partnerDesc, y = quantity_bbl))+
  geom_col()+
  labs(title = "Top 20 US Crude Oil Export Destinations (2023)",
       x = "Partners",
       y = "Annual Barrels (BBL)",
       caption = "*Data from UN Comtrade Database 2023 Data"
       )+
  theme_bw()+
  coord_flip()
```

# Graph 2: Oil Exports vs Life Expectancy

```{r}
library(fuzzyjoin)
library(plotly)
hpi_df <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/highamm/ds234_quarto/main/data_online/hpi-tidy.csv")

global_export_region <- stringdist_full_join(
  hpi_df,
  global_exports_clean,
  by = c("Country" = "reporterDesc"),
  max_dist = 2
)
# graph 2: Relating oil exports to life expectancy

global_export_region1 <- global_export_region %>%
  filter(Importer == "W00")%>%
  filter(quantity_bbl > 0)
  
region_plot <- ggplot(data = global_export_region1, aes(x = LifeExpectancy, y = quantity_bbl, color = Region, label = Country))+
  geom_point()+
  theme_bw()+
  scale_color_viridis_d()+
  labs(title = "Life Expectancy Based on Crude Oil Exports",
       x = " Life Expectancy",
       y = "Barrels Exported",
       caption = "*data from 2023 UN Comtrade Data & Happy Planet Index")

ggplotly(p = region_plot, tooltip = "label")
```

# Graph 3: Network Graph

```{r}
library(rvest)
library(tidygraph)
library(ggraph)


network_graph_data <- global_export_region %>%
  rename(
    from = reporterDesc,
    to = partnerDesc,
    barrels = quantity_bbl
  )

nodes <- network_graph_data %>%
  select(to, from, Country, Region)%>%
  filter(!is.na(from) & !is.na(to)) %>%
  distinct(Country, Region)%>%
  slice(-89)%>%
  rename(name = Country)

valid_nodes <- nodes$name

edges <- left_join(network_graph_data, nodes, by = join_by(Country == name)) %>%
  filter(to != "World") %>%
  select(from, to, barrels) %>%
  filter(!is.na(from) & !is.na(to))

# the following came from Claude, with the prompt : why am I getting an error in my edgelist saying there are NA's present, when I have checked for NA values?

edges <- edges %>%
  filter(from %in% valid_nodes & to %in% valid_nodes)

# End of Claude code

network_obj <- tbl_graph(nodes = nodes, edges = edges, directed = FALSE)

library(ggraph)
ggraph(network_obj |> mutate(centrality = centrality_authority()),
                            layout = "fr") +
  geom_edge_link(aes(width = barrels), alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_node_point(aes(colour = Region,
                      size = centrality), show.legend = TRUE) +
  scale_edge_width(range = c(0.5, 3)) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE)+
  scale_colour_viridis_d()
```

# Future Work:

If time was not a constraint for me, I would have liked to look at trade networks over time, maybe while controlling for a select few countries. I'd like to see how different key players in oil trade have changed with whom they trade with over time, and try and explore why those changes may have occured.

Some limitations for me during this project included my data. It was tough for me to clean my data in a way that made it easy to work with, and it was even harder to join my dataset with another while still keeping relevant variables. I wish I knew a little bit more about cleaning and joining datasets, as that would make it easier for me to continue to incorporate additional data sets to look at even more variables that could enhance my analysis.
